title: Gymnocarpium × brittonianum
intro: Gymnocarpium × brittonianum, also known as the Britton's oak fern, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the Osmundaceae family. The Britton's oak fern has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Britton's oak fern is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and promote general well-being.
taxonomy: Gymnocarpium × brittonianum is a hybrid species of fern in the family Matoniaceae. It is a result of a cross between Gymnocarpium dryopteris and Gymnocarpium juluceum. The classification of this plant is that it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Matoniaceae. Gymnocarpium × brittonianum is further divided into the genus Gymnocarpium and the species brittonianum. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup.
uses: Gymnocarpium × brittonianum, also known as Britton's oak fern or Britton's wood fern, is a rare hybrid of two different species of ferns.
